pattern: When spawning 2+ workers via /swarm or /codex-team without an enforced edit-scope lock, file-collision is the #1 swarm failure mode (see CLAUDE.md: 'Before spawning workers: Verify no file overlap'). gstack's /freeze + /guard + /unfreeze ships a hard-block edit-scope mechanism that PreToolUse hooks can enforce — pattern worth porting before the next swarm primitive lands.
detection_question: Does agentops have a hard-block mechanism preventing edits outside a declared swarm-wave scope, not just an advisory?
checklist_item: Add /scope freeze (skill) + hooks/edit-scope-guard.sh (PreToolUse) before any new multi-worker swarm primitive ships.

pattern: State paths under .agents/ should resolve through a single library (lib/ao-paths.sh + cli/internal/paths/), not be recomputed inline by each hook and Go callsite. gstack's bin/gstack-paths is the canonical example: one file owns env-var precedence (CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA > GSTACK_HOME > default). Without this, path semantics drift across consumers and refactors break in dark corners.
detection_question: Are .agents/ subpaths resolved through one library, or recomputed in each consumer (hook script, Go module, skill)?
checklist_item: Audit hooks/*.sh and cli/cmd/ao/*.go for ad-hoc '.agents/' string concatenation. New hooks/subcommands MUST source the resolver, not hardcode paths.

pattern: Skill bundles that lack a browser-validation lane have a structural blind spot: post-deploy live-site issues (broken CSS, OAuth flow regression, runtime JS errors) are invisible to test/lint/typecheck. gstack's /browse + /qa + /canary lane addresses this. Agentops users today are mostly CLI/library authors so the gap is small, but flagging the design decision keeps it visible if/when web-surface skills land.
detection_question: Can the operator validate post-deploy UI from inside the agent loop, or are HTTP-served surfaces validated only via test code?
checklist_item: Before declaring validation complete on a web-served change, confirm a /browse-equivalent live probe ran. If no such skill exists, flag as an open design question rather than silently passing.

pattern: When a Go-CLI mass refactor touches paths embedded into the binary (cli/embedded/), surface enumeration MUST include the embedded tree and acceptance MUST require make sync-hooks re-run. Otherwise the embedded copies drift silently from the source-of-truth tree.
detection_question: Does the refactor surface include cli/embedded/, OR does the plan's acceptance command list `cd cli && make sync-hooks`?
checklist_item: For any refactor touching hooks/, lib/, or skills/ paths that ship in the binary: add cli/embedded/ to the enumerated surface AND require `make sync-hooks` in conformance commands.

pattern: Porting a hook from another tool must include an L2 test that verifies the hook FIRES post-install — simulate the harness's stdin contract, assert non-zero on block path AND zero on allow path. `bash -n` is insufficient because it only validates parse, not behavior or registration.
detection_question: Does the hook port include an L2 test that simulates harness stdin and asserts both block (non-zero) and allow (zero) outcomes?
checklist_item: For any new hook script: add tests/hooks/test-<hook>-fires.sh that simulates input JSON and asserts both reject and accept paths. `bash -n` alone fails this check.

pattern: Hooks reading structured input from stdin MUST declare fail-open vs fail-closed behavior on malformed input explicitly. Default fail-open for advisory hooks (don't block; log warning); fail-closed only for security-critical hooks with audit logging. Silent crash on malformed input == blocks all work.
detection_question: Does the hook spec explicitly handle malformed input (missing JSON keys, jq parse failure, empty stdin)?
checklist_item: For any hook reading JSON stdin: add an explicit malformed-input branch with a documented fail-open or fail-closed decision. Test with `echo garbage | hook` and assert the documented behavior.
